title: Cheap-stop and undercut opportunity proxies from peer context
summary: Public F1 strategy references emphasize pit timing as a function of pit-loss discount, traffic, undercut/overcut viability, and caution windows.
rationale: Public F1 strategy references emphasize pit timing as a function of pit-loss discount, traffic, undercut/overcut viability, and caution windows. Even without explicit SC/VSC labels, race-lap peer dispersion can proxy when a stop is unusually attractive or defensible.
implementation_hint: Engineer race-lap contextual features such as: driver gap-to-race-lap median pace, percentile of tyre age within current lap, fraction of peers already on older/newer tyres, pit-wave intensity in previous 1-2 laps, outlier lap-time delta spikes indicating incidents, and estimated cheap-stop score from sudden compression in field pace dispersion. Interact these with position and compound.
expected_effect: Adds competitive context missing from solo tyre-life rules, especially around pit waves and opportunistic stops.
risk: Context features built with same-lap aggregates can leak if they accidentally use future-ordered rows; must rely only on contemporaneous race-lap information available for all cars.
